Equivalent (JA): 幾何ドメインライブラリ
Definition (EN): The Geometry Domain Library contains a general library model of physical items with spatial extent and a library model of specific spatial items that represent basic geometric shapes.
Context/Example (EN): The Geometry Domain Library contains a general library model of physical items with spatial extent and a library model of specific spatial items that represent basic geometric shapes.

Definition (EN): A SpatialItem is an Item with a three-dimensional spatial extent that also acts as a SpatialFrame of reference.
Context/Example (EN): A SpatialItem is an Item with a three-dimensional spatial extent that also acts as a SpatialFrame of reference.

Definition (EN): The CurrentDisplacementOf two Points relative to a SpatialItem and a Clock is the DisplacementOf the Points relative to the SpacialItem, at the currentTime of the Clock.
Context/Example (EN): The CurrentDisplacementOf two Points relative to a SpatialItem and a Clock is the DisplacementOf the Points relative to the SpacialItem, at the currentTime of the Clock.

Definition (EN): The CurrentPositionOf a Point relative to a SpatialItem and a Clock is the PositionOf the Point relative to the SpatialItem at the currentTime of the Clock.
Context/Example (EN): The CurrentPositionOf a Point relative to a SpatialItem and a Clock is the PositionOf the Point relative to the SpatialItem at the currentTime of the Clock.

Definition (EN): The DisplacementOf two Points relative to a SpatialItem, at a specific TimeInstantValue relative to a given Clock, is the displacementVector computed as the difference between the PositionOf the first Point and PositionOf the second Point, relative to that SpatialItem, at that timeInstant
Context/Example (EN): The DisplacementOf two Points relative to a SpatialItem, at a specific time relative to a given Clock, is the displacementVector computed as the difference between the PositionOf the first Point and PositionOf the second Point, relative to that SpatialItem, at that time

Definition (EN): The PositionOf a Point relative to a SpatialItem, at a specific TimeInstantValue relative to a given Clock, as a positionVector that is a VectorQuantityValue in the coordinateFrame of the SpatialItem. The default Clock is the localClock of the SpatialItem.
Context/Example (EN): The PositionOf a Point relative to a SpatialItem, at a specific TimeInstantValue relative to a given Clock, as a positionVector that is a VectorQuantityValue in the coordinateFrame of the SpatialItem. The default Clock is the localClock of the SpatialItem.

Definition (EN): This package defines basic geometric Items, most of which can be used as shapes of other Items (see 7.10 ). Their innerSpaceDimensions are either 1 (Curves) or 2 (Surfaces), which is the number of variables needed to identify any space point occupied by an Item, without regard to higher dimensional spaces in which it might be embedded (see Occurrences and Objects in [KerML]). All are StructuredSpaceObjects (Paths and Shells for Curves and Surfaces, respectively), except for Lines. This enables them to divide their spaceSlices into faces, edges, and vertices, identifying Surfaces, Curve, and Points, respectively. Paths have no faces, but Shells do. All the Paths and Shells are closed (isClosed=true, they have no shape), except for Discs, enabling them to be shapes of other Items.
Context/Example (EN): This package defines basic geometric Items, most of which can be used as shapes of other Items (see 7.10 ).

Definition (EN): A Circle is an Ellipse with semiaxes equal to its radius.
Context/Example (EN): A Circle is an Ellipse with semiaxes equal to its radius.

Definition (EN): A CircularCone is a Cone with a circular base.
Context/Example (EN): A CircularCone is a Cone with a circular base.

Definition (EN): A CircularCylinder is a Cylinder with two circular sides.
Context/Example (EN): A CircularCylinder is a Cylinder with two circular sides.

Definition (EN): A CircularDisc is a Disc bound by a Circle.
Context/Example (EN): A CircularDisc is a Disc bound by a Circle.

Definition (EN): A Cone has one elliptical sides joined to a point by a curved side.
Context/Example (EN): A Cone has one elliptical sides joined to a point by a curved side.

Definition (EN): A ConeOrCylinder is a Cone or a Cylinder with a given elliptical base, height, width (perpendicular distance from the base to the center of the top side or vertex), and offsets of this perpendicular at the base from the center of the base.
Context/Example (EN): A ConeOrCylinder is a Cone or a Cylinder with a given elliptical base, height, width (perpendicular distance from the base to the center of the top side or vertex), and offsets of this perpendicular at the base from the center of the base.

Definition (EN): A ConicSection is a closed PlanarCurve, possibly disconnected, see Hyperbola.
Context/Example (EN): A ConicSection is a closed PlanarCurve, possibly disconnected, see Hyperbola.

Definition (EN): A ConicSurface is a Surface that has ConicSection cross-sections.
Context/Example (EN): A ConicSurface is a Surface that has ConicSection cross-sections.

Definition (EN): A Cuboid is a Polyhedron with six sides, all quadrilateral.
Context/Example (EN): A Cuboid is a Polyhedron with six sides, all quadrilateral.

Definition (EN): A CuboidOrTriangularPrism is a Polyhedron that is either a Cuboid or TriangularPrism.
Context/Example (EN): A CuboidOrTriangularPrism is a Polyhedron that is either a Cuboid or TriangularPrism.

Definition (EN): A Cylinder has two elliptical sides joined by a curved side.
Context/Example (EN): A Cylinder has two elliptical sides joined by a curved side.

Definition (EN): A Disc is a Shell bound by an Ellipse.
Context/Example (EN): A Disc is a Shell bound by an Ellipse.

Definition (EN): An EccentricCone is a Cone with least one positive offset.
Context/Example (EN): An EccentricCone is a Cone with least one positive offset.

Definition (EN): An EccentricCylinder is a Cylinder
Context/Example (EN): An EccentricCylinder is a Cylinder

Definition (EN): An Ellipse is a ConicSection in the shape of an ellipse of given semiaxes.
Context/Example (EN): An Ellipse is a ConicSection in the shape of an ellipse of given semiaxes.

Definition (EN): An Ellipsoid is a ConicSurface with only elliptical cross-sections.
Context/Example (EN): An Ellipsoid is a ConicSurface with only elliptical cross-sections.

Definition (EN): A Hyperbola is a planar Path in the shape of a hyperbola with given axes.
Context/Example (EN): A Hyperbola is a planar Path in the shape of a hyperbola with given axes.

Definition (EN): A Hyperboloid is a ConicSurface with only hyperbolic cross-sections.
Context/Example (EN): A Hyperboloid is a ConicSurface with only hyperbolic cross-sections.

Definition (EN): A Line is a PlanarCurve that is straight.
Context/Example (EN): A Line is a PlanarCurve that is straight.

Definition (EN): A Parabola is a planar Path in the shape of a parabola of a given focal length.
Context/Example (EN): A Parabola is a planar Path in the shape of a parabola of a given focal length.

Definition (EN): A Paraboloid is a ConicSurface with only parabolic cross-sections.
Context/Example (EN): A Paraboloid is a ConicSurface with only parabolic cross-sections.

Definition (EN): Path is the most general structured Curve.
Context/Example (EN): Path is the most general structured Curve.

Definition (EN): A PlanarCurve is a Curve with a given length embeddable in a plane.
Context/Example (EN): A PlanarCurve is a Curve with a given length embeddable in a plane.

Definition (EN): A PlanarSurface is a Surface with given area that is flat.
Context/Example (EN): A PlanarSurface is a Surface with given area that is flat.

Definition (EN): A Polygon is a closed planar Path with straight edges.
Context/Example (EN): A Polygon is a closed planar Path with straight edges.

Definition (EN): A Polyhedron is a closed Shell with polygonal sides.
Context/Example (EN): A Polyhedron is a closed Shell with polygonal sides.

Definition (EN): A Pyramid is a Polyhedron with the sides of a polygon (base) forming the bases of triangles that join at an apex point. Its height is the perpindicular distance from the base to the apex, and its offsets are between this perpindicular at the base and the center of the base.
Context/Example (EN): A Pyramid is a Polyhedron with the sides of a polygon (base) forming the bases of triangles that join at an apex point.

Definition (EN): A Quadrilateral is a four-sided Polygon.
Context/Example (EN): A Quadrilateral is a four-sided Polygon.

Definition (EN): A Rectangle is a Quadrilateral with four right angles and given length and width.
Context/Example (EN): A Rectangle is a Quadrilateral with four right angles and given length and width.

Definition (EN): A RectangularCuboid is a Cuboid with all Rectangular sides.
Context/Example (EN): A RectangularCuboid is a Cuboid with all Rectangular sides.

Definition (EN): A RectangularPyramid is a Pyramid with a rectangular base.
Context/Example (EN): A RectangularPyramid is a Pyramid with a rectangular base.

Definition (EN): A RectangularToroid is a revolution of a Rectangle.
Context/Example (EN): A RectangularToroid is a revolution of a Rectangle.

Definition (EN): A RightCircularCone is a CircularCone with zero offsets.
Context/Example (EN): A RightCircularCone is a CircularCone with zero offsets.

Definition (EN): A RightCircularCylinder is a CircularCylinder with zero offsets.
Context/Example (EN): A RightCircularCylinder is a CircularCylinder with zero offsets.

Definition (EN): A RightTriangle is a Triangle with edges opposite the hypotenuse at right angles.
Context/Example (EN): A RightTriangle is a Triangle with edges opposite the hypotenuse at right angles.

Definition (EN): A RightTriangularPrism is a TriangularPrism with two right triangular sides, with given length, width, and height.
Context/Example (EN): A RightTriangularPrism is a TriangularPrism with two right triangular sides, with given length, width, and height.

Definition (EN): Shell is the most general structured Surface.
Context/Example (EN): Shell is the most general structured Surface.

Definition (EN): A Sphere is an Ellipsoid with all the same semiaxes.
Context/Example (EN): A Sphere is an Ellipsoid with all the same semiaxes.

Definition (EN): A Tetrahedron is Pyramid with a triangular base.
Context/Example (EN): A Tetrahedron is Pyramid with a triangular base.

Definition (EN): A Toroid is a surface generated from revolving a planar closed curve about an line coplanar with the curve. It is single sided with one hole.
Context/Example (EN): A Toroid is a surface generated from revolving a planar closed curve about an line coplanar with the curve.

Definition (EN): A Torus is a revolution of a Circle.
Context/Example (EN): A Torus is a revolution of a Circle.

Definition (EN): A Triangle is three-sided Polygon with given length (base), width (perpindicular distance from base to apex), and offset of this perpindicular at the base from the center of the base.
Context/Example (EN): A Triangle is three-sided Polygon with given length (base), width (perpindicular distance from base to apex), and offset of this perpindicular at the base from the center of the base.

Definition (EN): A TriangularPrism is a Polyhedron with five sides, two triangular and the others quadrilateral.
Context/Example (EN): A TriangularPrism is a Polyhedron with five sides, two triangular and the others quadrilateral.
